chars.meaning: Aspiration, resolve — the direction the heart is set on; “An army can lose its commander, but a person cannot be made to surrender their purpose” (Analects)
Soaring, head held high — high spirits, forward momentum
Heroic grandeur; Su Shi’s lines: “Rolling east, the Yangtze sweeps a thousand heroes away”
Tenacity, iron perseverance — the character is built from stand and strike: standing firm under pressure
Courage, the willingness to step forward; “A sense of shame is close to courage” (Doctrine of the Mean)
Open-hearted uprightness — “bright and open-hearted” is a stock Chinese idiom
Lofty peaks, towering aspiration — the character of a craggy, noble landscape
Soaring, racing, a leap into flight — “the dragon rises over the four seas”
chars.suits: Those full of drive and ambition

chars.meaning: To soar with spread wings, freely and without constraint; Zhuangzi’s “the great bird on its journey to the southern ocean”
chars.suits: Those who value freedom and open horizons

chars.meaning: Setting out on a great journey, pioneering — implies enterprise and bold departure
Grand vision, sweeping ambition — a character of breadth and scale
Majestic, great — Peter Hessler’s Chinese given name (何伟) shows how well this works: direct and strong
chars.suits: Those who want to leave a lasting mark

chars.meaning: To fly, to soar on aspiration; “the flying dragon in the heavens” (Book of Changes)
chars.suits: The ambitious, those restless with the ordinary

chars.meaning: To surpass, to cross over — the impulse to clear every obstacle
To exert oneself, to press on without yielding; “Heaven moves with strength; the noble person never ceases self-renewal” (Book of Changes)
To rise above the summit; Du Fu: “When at the very top, one sees all the mountains below”
chars.suits: Those who aim for the peak and refuse to stop short

chars.meaning: Humaneness, benevolent care for others — the core of Confucian ethics; “Humaneness is to love others” (Analects)
Righteousness, acting in accordance with what is right — the Confucian question of principle over profit
Integrity, keeping one’s word — one of the Five Confucian Constants
Sincerity, genuine truthfulness — first of the four qualities in the Ho family’s motto (Sincerity · Reverence · Care · Precision)
Reverence, composed attentiveness — second of the four Ho family qualities
Integrity in the face of temptation — incorruptibility; Confucius: “廉而不刿” (upright without cutting)
chars.suits: Those who are principled and self-disciplined

chars.meaning: Steadfastness, staying the course; the Book of Changes Heng hexagram: “Constancy — lasting”
chars.suits: The resilient, those who persist through time

chars.meaning: Humility, the capacity to receive; “The modest noble person may cross great waters” (Book of Changes)
Loyalty, wholehearted commitment — the character is built from heart: what you show outside mirrors what is inside
chars.suits: Those loyal to their beliefs and their people

chars.meaning: Gracious goodness, gentle virtue; “A fair and gracious young woman” (Book of Odes) — conventional feminine resonance
Refinement, cultivated grace — one of the three sections of the Book of Odes
chars.suits: Those with an artistic or literary sensibility

chars.meaning: Proper, composed — correct in bearing and conduct
Goodness, the impulse to do good; “The highest good is like water” (Dao De Jing)
Solid, devoted, deeply committed to learning; “Earnest in belief, eager in learning, steadfast in the good way” (Analects)
Empathy, the practice of putting yourself in others’ shoes; “Do not impose on others what you would not choose for yourself” (Analects)
Upright fidelity to one’s true nature; “Correct and steadfast — sufficient to undertake affairs” (Book of Changes)
chars.suits: Those who hold their ground without drifting

chars.meaning: Mountain — stable, immovable; “The humane love mountains” (Analects)
Mountain mist at dawn — the ethereal light vapour that rises between peaks at first light
Rivers flowing without cease, all waters returning to the sea; “Does it not flow on like this, never stopping?” (Analects)
The great river in full flood — Su Shi’s verse evokes its sweeping, relentless grandeur
chars.suits: Men of broad vision and expansive spirit

chars.meaning: Free-floating clouds, a crane in the mist — unhurried, unconstrained
A clean wind, the “wind-character” of a person (bearing, style); Zhuangzi on the great wind needing depth to carry a great wing
chars.suits: The graceful, those with a distinctive personal style

chars.meaning: Spring rain that nourishes — Du Fu: “The good rain knows its season” — gentle and life-giving; works well as a unisex choice
Untouched snow — pure, austere, cool; “Snow makes the plum blossom look three times whiter” (Lu Meipo)
chars.suits: Women of purity and quiet independence

chars.meaning: The moon — soft, reflective luminosity; the central image of the Mid-Autumn Festival and countless poems of longing
Dawn — the moment of renewal, the world beginning again; one of the most popular unisex given-name characters today
The orchid in the secluded valley — “The orchid blooms in an empty valley, unfazed by having no audience” (Confucius)
Bamboo — hollow-hearted (humble), upright-jointed (principled); “Better no meat at table than no bamboo by the wall” (Su Shi)
chars.suits: Those with moral backbone and natural elegance

chars.meaning: The pine and cypress that outlast winter; “Only when the year grows cold do we know the pine and cypress are the last to shed their leaves” (Analects)
Surging waves — Su Shi: “Startled waves crash on the shore, rolling up a thousand snowdrifts”
chars.suits: Men of sweeping energy and presence

chars.meaning: To moisten, to nourish, to benefit — grace spreading outward like water seeping into soil
The rising sun at dawn, vigorous morning light
Light, radiance — carrying civilization forward; the author’s own studio name (承天, Receiving Heaven) resonates in the same field
Brilliant and full-blazing — the character joins sun and accomplish: light and achievement in one
chars.suits: Those with clear goals and ambition (conventionally masculine)

chars.meaning: The first light of dawn — the word itself has a delicate sound that suits its meaning
The lingering warmth of late sunlight — Wen Tingyun: “The slanting light, pulse by quiet pulse, the water flowing on”
chars.suits: The quietly devoted, the long-running flame

chars.meaning: Brilliant white, the brightness of the full moon across miles of still water
Clear and resonant — “a clear wind and bright day” describes both weather and character
Clear-flowing, incorruptibly clean; Li Bai: “The lotus rises from clear water, naturally, without ornament”
To soar beyond convention — the free spirit, at ease in the world; Wang Bo: “Exhilaration lifts, and I fly”
Settled clarity — water that has stilled until you can see the bottom; Wang Wei’s “clear spring running over stone”
chars.suits: Those of calm, untroubled inner clarity

chars.meaning: High purity — the lotus that grows from mud and emerges spotless (Zhou Dunyi, Love of the Lotus)
chars.suits: Women of high moral character (conventionally feminine)

chars.meaning: Like water — from “The highest good is like water” (Dao De Jing): yielding, flowing, taking the shape of every vessel
chars.suits: The soft and adaptive, those with a Daoist sensibility

chars.meaning: Stillness, the quiet depth beneath a calm surface; “Reach the utmost emptiness; hold fast to stillness” (Dao De Jing)
Serene detachment, freedom from craving; “Seek serenity in order to extend ambition’s reach” (Zhuge Liang)
chars.suits: Those who live above the noise of ambition and reputation

chars.meaning: Far-reaching vision, thought bridging millennia; “The high mountain, one looks up to it” (Book of Odes) — good unisex choice
Quiet depth, the fragrance of the orchid in solitude; “An orchid in the front courtyard” (Tao Yuanming)
chars.suits: The introvert who finds beauty in privacy (conventionally feminine)

chars.meaning: The clearing after rain — clouds parting, the moon breaking through; “A clearing moon is rare to meet” — a metaphor for hard-won serenity
chars.suits: Those who emerge into openness after difficulty; unisex

chars.meaning: Wide learning, broad knowledge; “Broad in learning and steadfast in purpose, close in questioning and close in thought” (Analects)
Intuitive wisdom, quick insight — the Buddhist prajña, the highest form of knowing
Deep, far-seeing intelligence — the word was classically used of emperors; it implies a quality of vision that goes further than ordinary cleverness
chars.suits: Those with exceptional depth of judgment

chars.meaning: Rational discernment — the Confucian virtue of zhi, the capacity to tell right from wrong; paired with ren (humaneness) and yi (righteousness)
Quick and alert, fast to learn; “Quick to learn and never ashamed to ask those who know less” (Analects)
Perceptive — the character is built from the ear radical, emphasizing the capacity to truly listen and understand
Philosophical wisdom, the sage’s capacity to know; “To know others is wisdom; to know yourself is enlightenment” (Dao De Jing)
Exceptional talent that breaks through the surface — the original meaning is the tip of a grain stalk; now it means to stand out
Nimble intelligence, flashes of inspiration — the Daoist value of spontaneous, unconstrained thought
Literary cultivation, the light of civilization — Kevin Rudd’s Chinese given name includes this character; it conveys a bookish, cultural quality
chars.suits: Those with literary or cultural pursuits

chars.meaning: To suddenly see — the Chan Buddhist moment of enlightenment, the “aha” of full comprehension
chars.suits: The philosophically inclined, those given to sudden deep understanding

chars.meaning: To reach through, to get there — “In times of constraint, perfect yourself; when success comes, carry the whole world with you” (Mencius)
To stand apart, to be genuinely distinguished — zhuo’er buqun (to stand alone and unbeholden)
chars.suits: Those who aim at distinction, not mere success (conventionally masculine)

chars.meaning: To think deeply, to sit with a question; “To study without reflection is wasted effort; to reflect without study is dangerous” (Analects)
To speak and be remembered — the second of the three forms of lasting achievement: establish virtue, establish deeds, establish words (Zuo Zhuan)
chars.suits: Writers, speakers, and those whose work lives in language

chars.meaning: Discernment, the ability to see truly — “Rare and penetrating discernment”: knowing what matters and what does not
Quiet peace, inner stillness; “Seek serenity to extend the reach of ambition” (Zhuge Liang)
chars.suits: Those who long for inner calm

chars.meaning: Joy, happiness that doesn’t spill over into excess; “Joyful without abandon, sorrowful without injury” (Analects)
Delight, exuberant gladness — the brightness of a good day in company
Contentment, the ease of a person at peace with where they are; “At ease and at home with life” (Tao Yuanming)
chars.suits: Those who find enough in what they have

chars.meaning: The quiet pleasure of learning, of understanding; Confucius: “Is it not a pleasure to study and practice what you learn?” (Analects; the original character 说 is the same word)
Settled safety, unshakable calm; “Be at peace yet never forget danger” (Book of Changes)
Health and abundance — a name that carries a blessing; “body and heart in good health” is the traditional wish
chars.suits: Those who want the name itself to carry a wish for wellbeing

chars.meaning: Grand ease, unruffled by events; Li Si: “Mount Tai does not refuse a single handful of earth — that is how it grows so great”
Good omen, auspicious arrival — “Auspicious harmony, filling all of heaven and earth”
chars.suits: Those who want a name with a blessing in it

chars.meaning: Fragrant warmth, the quality of a presence that lingers; Cao Zhi: “How sweet the fragrance drifts”
chars.suits: The warm, the kind-hearted, the giver of comfort

chars.meaning: To nourish, to tend the self; the Book of Changes Yi hexagram: “Heaven and earth nourish the ten thousand things”
chars.suits: Those who value balance and self-care

chars.meaning: To blend, to harmonize — “Heaven and earth were born with me; the ten thousand things and I are one” (Zhuangzi)
chars.suits: Peacemakers and those who draw people together

chars.meaning: Warmth and light, everything flourishing; “All under heaven is stirring — all for gain” (the phrase is ironic, but 熙 alone carries only the warmth of its image)
Unyielding strength, moral backbone — the character of someone who does not bend when it matters; “The strong and the yielding push against each other; change lives in between” (Book of Changes)
chars.suits: Men who are upright and principled

chars.meaning: The ring of metal — “the iron backbone” is a stock phrase; the sound the character names evokes clarity and resonance
chars.suits: Those with integrity who cannot be bought

chars.meaning: The dragon — the Chinese emblem of auspicious power and celestial energy; “the flying dragon in the heavens” (Book of Changes). Note: In many Western traditions the dragon is a creature of menace; if using this character with non-Chinese audiences, a brief cultural note is worth offering
A thunderclap — the speed and force of lightning; “Thunder before you can cover your ears”
Grand strategy, far-ranging vision; “Showing wisdom, showing deeds” (Book of Documents) — a statesman’s character
Fierce valor, the quality of an outstanding warrior
Boundless as a desert sea; Wang Wei: “The lone smoke rises straight in the great desert; the long river’s sunset round and whole”
To engrave, to inscribe — what is cut into metal or stone to be read by those who come after
chars.suits: Those who want to leave something lasting

chars.meaning: To receive, to carry forward — the author’s own studio name (承天, Receiving Heaven) embodies this: inheriting and passing on
chars.suits: Those with a sense of mission and heritage

chars.meaning: Imperious composure, an aura not to be trifled with — “upright and not to be deceived”
chars.suits: Those of natural authority and gravitas

chars.meaning: Lofty and open — the bearing of a gentleman who inhabits space with ease; “a great wave rising” describes the sweep of it
chars.suits: Men of striking presence and natural ease

chars.meaning: Martial virtue — but the character’s etymology runs deeper: zhi ge (止戈), to stop the spear — the truest strength ends conflict rather than starting it
chars.suits: The strong who use their strength wisely

chars.meaning: Graceful posture, the upright beauty of a figure lightly poised; “growing upright and apart, purely itself” (Love of the Lotus)
Delicately wrought — like a jewel whose facets catch every light
chars.suits: Women who are clever, appealing, and finely made

chars.meaning: Precious jade, the music of jade; “The Queen Mother of the West opens her jade-latticed window” (Li Shangyin)
chars.suits: Women of high-born grace and a hint of the otherworldly

chars.meaning: The madder plant’s red — vivid, warm, a saturated color that catches the eye
chars.suits: Women who are vivid, lively, and full of warm color

chars.meaning: Morning clouds blazing with color; Wang Bo: “A lone swan flies with the autumn waters into one” — that same sky
A fine piece of jade — smooth, warm, quietly precious; the jade radical marks this as a character of refined feminine elegance
The plum blossom that pushes through snow — Wang Anshi: “You know it isn’t snow — it has that hidden fragrance”
chars.suits: Independent, resilient women with inner strength

chars.meaning: The lotus blossom, pure above muddy water; shares its sound with the author’s surname 何 — particularly fitting for anyone surnamed He
The lotus bud not yet opened — handan, the ancient word for the half-closed flower; Li Jing: “The lotus fragrance fades, the jade leaves are spent”
A warm reddish glow — cloud color at dusk, the blush of firelight
chars.suits: The warm, the generous, the person who brings color into a room

chars.meaning: Blazing brilliance — the jade radical with light: a gemstone at full radiance
chars.suits: Women of dazzling talent and presence

chars.meaning: Softness that is also strength; the Dao De Jing: “Nothing in the world is softer than water — yet nothing beats it at overcoming the hard and strong”
chars.suits: Women who are outwardly gentle but inwardly unbreakable

chars.meaning: A smile that takes everything: “One glance, a hundred beauties” (Bai Juyi)
chars.suits: Women whose charm is effortless and magnetic

chars.meaning: Rue-herb fragrance — the delicate scent of old books and gardens; understated and literary
chars.suits: Women of quiet refinement with a bookish, fragrant quality

chars.meaning: Excellence, beauty, the best of something — the very character in this book’s Chinese title (Wo You Jia Ming: I Have a Fine Name)
chars.suits: Those who carry all the best qualities at once

chars.meaning: A classical particle turned given name — used by Wang Xizhi (calligrapher) and Li Daoyuan (geographer); pure literary atmosphere
chars.suits: Those with a taste for classical Chinese culture

chars.meaning: The cosmos — to hold the universe in one’s bearing; “the breath swallows rivers and mountains, the voice exhales the cosmos”
Brilliance, flower-like beauty, civilization at its best — talent and cultural inheritance in a single character
A fine piece of jade — warm, precious, and quietly luminous; increasingly used as a gender-neutral character
chars.suits: Those of inner quality and gentle warmth

chars.meaning: Natural, as things are — the Daoist principle of ziran (自然, following what is natural)
The beginning, the original heart; “Never forget where you started — and you will get where you are going”
chars.suits: Those who hold to their first intentions

chars.meaning: A feather, a wing — light, free, effortlessly in motion; “The swan gradually shows its feathers — fine conduct in full view” (Book of Changes)
The maple — red leaves in deep autumn: vivid, warm, with a note of melancholy and depth
chars.suits: Those with a striking personality and a taste for the poetic

chars.meaning: To be bathed in — rain, wind, sunlight, grace; grateful to whatever nourishes you
chars.suits: The grateful, those who know what they have received

chars.meaning: Precious, to cherish
Auspicious, a lucky omen
Flourishing, vibrant
Books, writing, literary culture
Enduring brilliance, outstanding intelligence
A person of integrity; a gentleman or gentlewoman
Celebration, joyous occasion
Heart, inner life
Graceful, outstanding
Bright, wise, luminous
People, humane regard for others
Precious, a treasure
Mist drifting lightly; poetic and ethereal
Virtue, moral character
The Way; principle, the path
Forest, grove; the world of learning
South; warmth, directional resonance
A person of talent and moral excellence
Source, origin, root
True, authentic, genuine
Harmony, accord; gentle balance among people and things
Glad, thriving; the joy of growth and new beginnings
Luminous, made manifest; bright and evident
The light just before dawn; promise of day
The morning hour, stars and planets; cosmic time
Learning, the lifelong pursuit of knowledge
To know, awareness and understanding
Ink; the art of brush and letters
The guqin; refinement and musical soul
Poetry; lyric beauty and feeling
Art, skill, cultivated craft
Cypress; evergreen steadfastness
Deep woods; abundance and depth
The sea; vast, all-embracing capacity
River; steady, nourishing flow
Sky, heaven; boundless aspiration
Star; a guiding, shining point
Frost; clear, resilient purity
Dew; fresh, delicate clarity
Spring of water; pure, living source
Abundant, flowing freely; ample vigor
Ocean, vast waters; expansive reach
Waves; movement and lively spirit
Vast and mighty, like floodwater; grandeur
Blessing, good fortune and well-being
Jade; warm virtue, flawless worth
Beautiful jade; lustrous and clear
Jade-like luster; crystalline brightness
Lotus; purity rising from the mud
Fragrance of flowers; lasting good name
Graceful, supple elegance
Beautiful, lovely; bright charm
Beautiful, radiant; fine and elegant
Beauty and goodness together
Strong, vigorous; force of will
Healthy vigor; tireless strength
Refined and cultured; gentlemanly grace
Handsome, talented; outstanding ability
Outstanding, heroic; a person of distinction
Propriety, ritual grace; respect made visible
Filial devotion; honoring one's roots
Uprightness, correctness; straight and true
Bright and shining; clear and open
Crystal-clear to the depths; transparent purity
Clean, pure stillness
To stand, to establish oneself with purpose
To accomplish, to become whole; fulfilment
To bring about, to devote oneself fully
Diligence; steady, willing effort
New, renewing; daily self-renewal
Sharp, keen; incisive drive
Swift, nimble; quick to succeed
Triumphant return; the song of victory
Victory, surpassing; rising above
Able, worthy, allowed; gentle possibility
Remembrance, mindful thought
As, like; accord and fulfilment ('as one wishes')
Evening tide; calm rhythmic grace
Mountain brook; clear, gentle flow
Fine jade's luster; excellence that shows
Precious jade; rare and fine
Fine jade, the extraordinary
Jade-white; pure brightness
To sail, to voyage; setting forth across distance
Sail; carried far on a fair wind
Boat; quiet passage across the water
A fine swift horse; spirited excellence
To gallop, to race ahead; momentum
Writing brush, letters; the literary
To moisten, enrich; gentle nourishing
Respectful admiration; held in esteem
Worthy and wise, of high virtue and ability
Good, fine, kind-hearted
Upright, square-dealing, principled
Straight and honest, true of heart
Simple, unadorned, genuine
Respectful, courteous, reverent
Broad-minded, generous, tolerant
Generous and substantial, deep in virtue
Warm, gentle, mild in manner
Compassionate, loving-kind
Sincere and generous, honest and thick in feeling
Harmonious and friendly, at ease with all
Gazing far, aspiring toward the distant
Awakened, aware, enlightened
Careful, prudent, conscientious
A fine emblem, beautiful and auspicious
Radiant, dazzling, illustrious
Brilliant, splendid, resplendent
Splendour, brilliant light
Made manifest, evident and distinguished
Clear sky, calm and bright
The rising sun, sunlight breaking through
The light of dawn, the sun about to rise
A high mountain, lofty and steadfast
A mountain peak, the summit
Rock, cliff — firm and enduring
The open plain, source and origin
The open wild, free and unbounded
An islet, a continent — vast reach
A lake, still and clear-hearted
A deep pool, clear and profound
Long-awaited nourishing rain
The high heavens, the empyrean
The vaulted sky, boundless dome
Great waves, sweeping and grand
Gentle ripples on water
A water's edge, a quiet sandbar
The paulownia tree, where the phoenix rests
Shade of wayside trees, cool repose
Luxuriant, flourishing, richly gathered
The nanmu, a fine and lasting hardwood
The banyan, broad-sheltering and evergreen
To hold aloft, to uphold
The great roc that flies ten thousand li
To soar straight up into the sky
Wings, support and aspiration
Kind and joyful, magnanimous
Great merit, distinguished achievement
Ardent, intense, upright and fierce
The blade's edge, keen and forward
To open up, to pioneer new ground
To temper and hone, self-discipline
Firm, resolute, unyielding
Surpassing, rising head and shoulders above
Glad, pleased, content of heart
Happy, cheerful, light of spirit
Free-flowing, unobstructed, at ease
Relaxed, unhurried, comfortably spread
Tranquil, quiet, untroubled
Still and serene, deeply quiet
Abundant, ample, well-provided
Abundant, plentiful, richly full
Full to abundance, brimming
Rich harvest, plenty
Gentle and graceful, tactfully lovely
Refined and poised, well-versed in grace
Beautiful, fair, a lovely woman
Wonderful, subtle, exquisitely fine
Beautiful, with a captivating grace
A beautiful and gifted woman
A sacred pearl of legend
A fine round jade tablet
Beautiful jade, lustrous
A fine jade, a bright star
Coral, delicate and precious
The fragrant orchid, pure and noble
The lotus / hibiscus, soft beauty
The rose / fern, tender and resilient
Soft grass, a verdant carpet
Lush and gently growing
Fitting, harmonious, well-suited
To study deeply, to grind fine
The ink-stone, the scholar's companion
The game of go, strategy and poise
A rare treasure, precious jade
A great jade disc of ceremony
A jade-like fine stone
A gem worn at the top of a jade pendant
A fine dappled steed
The qilin, an auspicious beast of virtue
The great wild goose; grand, far-reaching
The crane, longevity and noble purity
The kite-hawk soaring the heights
The oriole, sweet-voiced spring bird
The swallow, herald of spring, nimble
Lush and thriving, abundant growth
The cornerstone timber, a pillar of support
Auspicious, blessed with good fortune
Happiness and blessing, festive joy
To rise, to soar upward
A plume, a mark of distinction

Fang: upright and broad; Fan: dignified; Fei: light on the ear (Fairbank’s choice)

The richest range: Li is the most common surname in China; Lin: fresh; Lu: bookish; Lu: classical

Ma: open and energetic; Mei: cool and high-minded; Mo: understated; Meng: dignified

Strategic depth, celebrated intellect
surnames.origin: A clan from Zhu city that merged with the Ge clan; Zhuge Liang, the brilliant strategist of the Three Kingdoms, made this surname synonymous with wisdom

surnames.register: Distinctive, chivalric, a hint of the knight-errant
surnames.origin: A Jin dynasty noble’s fief in Linghu — the family took the place as its name

surname_by_sound.notes: Bai: bright and clean; Bao: direct and frank; Bei: luminous
Chen: one of the great common surnames; Cui: crisp and austere; Cen: quietly refined
Du: poetic (the poet Du Fu); Ding: spare and clean; Deng: steady
Fang: upright and broad; Fan: dignified; Fei: light on the ear (Fairbank’s choice)
Gao: open and elevated; Gu: warm, associated with the south; Ge: ancient and plain
He: friendly and natural (Hessler’s choice); Han: firm; Hua: radiant
Jin: precious; Jiang (江): clear and far-reaching; Jiang (姜): ancient flavor
Kong: noble (Confucius’s surname — pair with an appropriately large given name); Ke: understated
The richest range: Li is the most common surname in China; Lin: fresh; Lu: bookish; Lu: classical
Ma: open and energetic; Mei: cool and high-minded; Mo: understated; Meng: dignified

Yang: open; Ye: delicate and clear; Yao: ancient beauty; O/Ou sounds — try Ouyang

compound_surnames.origin: An ancient Zhou dynasty official title (Situ: minister of the people, keeper of education and rites)
compound_surnames.sound_match: St-/Stu- (the classic precedent: Leighton Stuart → 司徒雷登)

compound_surnames.origin: A prince of the Spring and Autumn state of Yue was enfeoffed at Ouyang — the family took the place name as its surname
compound_surnames.register: Southern literati elegance, classical in feel

compound_surnames.origin: A ministerial title in the state of Chu; the poet Qu Yuan’s friend Shangguan Dafu bore this name
compound_surnames.register: Noble and weighty, deep historical resonance

compound_surnames.origin: Sima: ancient minister of war; the Grand Historian Sima Qian, author of the Records of the Grand Historian, made this name legendary
A clan from Zhu city that merged with the Ge clan; Zhuge Liang, the brilliant strategist of the Three Kingdoms, made this surname synonymous with wisdom
A Jin dynasty noble’s fief in Linghu — the family took the place as its name
compound_surnames.register: Distinctive, chivalric, a hint of the knight-errant

men.approach: “Mi-” echoes ming (bright); kai carries the sense of triumphant return
men.sense: Radiant integrity, moving forward in triumph

men.approach: “Da-” echoes da (great); “vid” resonates with wei (to act), balancing sound and sense
men.sense: Grand ambition, making one’s mark

men.approach: “Ja-” echoes jia (fine, excellent); ming means an inscription handed down through the ages
men.sense: A beautiful name inscribed in memory

men.approach: “Dan-” echoes dan (vermilion, loyalty); yang carries brightness and warmth
men.sense: A loyal heart turned toward the sun, open and alive

men.approach: “E-” echoes yi (easy, adaptable); han suggests depth and inner cultivation
men.sense: Adaptable and at ease, with great depth of character

men.approach: “He-” echoes heng (enduring); rui conveys wisdom and clear insight
men.sense: Steady perseverance, exceptional depth of understanding

men.approach: “Wi-” echoes wen (culture, letters); lian suggests integrity and refinement
men.sense: Distinguished in learning, incorruptible in character

men.approach: “Tho-” echoes tian (heaven); ming means a legacy carved for posterity
men.sense: Standing tall between heaven and earth, a name for the ages

men.approach: The compound surname Ouyang echoes “Ol-”; li means to establish oneself with purpose
men.sense: A learned family tradition, ambition set high

men.approach: “Ben-” echoes ben (origin, root); ming brings light to the source
men.sense: Returning to what is essential, the original heart shining clear

men.approach: “Al-” echoes an (peace, steadiness); jun evokes a fine horse at full gallop
men.sense: Grounded and composed, with talent that surpasses the ordinary

men.approach: “An-” directly gives an (steady); rui suggests keenness and forward drive
men.sense: Calm at the core, sharp and enterprising

men.approach: “Na-” echoes nan (south); xuan suggests an upright, expansive bearing
men.sense: The aspiration of the southern mountain, a bearing full of dignity

men.approach: “Sam-” echoes si (to think, to consider); yuan means far-reaching in vision
men.sense: Thoughtful and reflective, with a far-reaching sense of purpose

men.approach: Follows the classical transliteration (established by Joseph Needham, Li Yuese); an alternative meaning-based rendering is 裕晟 (Yu Sheng)
men.sense: Abundance and flourishing, luminous in achievement

men.approach: “Cha-” echoes cheng (to carry on, to inherit); li evokes ritual, civility, and culture
men.sense: Inheriting a living tradition, passing on the rites of civilization

men.approach: “Geo-” echoes gao (high, lofty); yuan conveys far-reaching aspiration
men.sense: Looking up to high mountains, the will set on distant horizons

men.approach: “Ed-” echoes yi (free, transcendent); de carries moral depth and virtue
men.sense: Transcending the ordinary, with a character grounded in virtue

men.approach: “Pe-” echoes the surname Pei; de means virtue and moral excellence (following the precedent of Peter Hessler, known in Chinese as He Wei)
men.sense: Noble character, elegant and courteous

men.approach: “Ro-” echoes rong (glory, flourishing); bo suggests broad learning and wide knowledge
men.sense: Honorable and radiant, learned across many fields

men.approach: “Ri-” echoes li (to stand upright); che conveys crystalline clarity
men.sense: Upright and principled, the mind clear as still water

men.approach: “Chr-” echoes cheng (inherit); yang means to carry forward and make great
men.sense: Receiving the best of what came before, carrying it further

men.approach: “Ma-” echoes ming (bright, clear); zhi brings discernment and wisdom
men.sense: Clear-eyed and judicious, wisdom beyond the ordinary

men.approach: “Jo-” echoes jiu (long-lasting); xiang evokes soaring flight and freedom
men.sense: Soaring freely for a long time, without bounds

men.approach: “Bri-” echoes bo (wide, abundant learning); ren suggests shouldering a mission with commitment
men.sense: Wide-ranging knowledge, the courage to take on great responsibilities

men.approach: “Pa-” echoes bo (broad); qing suggests moral clarity and rectitude
men.sense: Broad in learning and knowledge, scrupulously upright

men.approach: “Ke-” echoes ke (capable, worthy); wen carries literary cultivation and refined culture (following Kevin Rudd, known in Chinese as Lu Kewen)
men.sense: Praiseworthy in letters, with a genuine love of learning

men.approach: “Ste-” echoes si (to think); wen brings cultural learning and literary gifts
men.sense: A deep love of study, outstanding in intellectual expression

men.approach: “Ma-” echoes ming (bright, clear); ming as inscription evokes a legacy handed down
men.sense: Radiant in character, leaving a mark that endures

men.approach: “Jon-” echoes jun (a person of integrity and cultivation); han suggests inclusiveness and depth
men.sense: The bearing of a true gentleman, with great depth of soul

women.approach: “Sa-” echoes si (thoughtful, reflective); ya conveys refinement and quiet distinction
women.sense: Thoughtful and far-seeing, with natural grace

women.approach: “Em-” echoes en (kindness, grace); mei brings beauty and goodness
women.sense: Kind and beautiful, warm in all her dealings

women.approach: Meaning-based: grace as blessing and good omen; jia means excellent and fine, rui means auspicious
women.sense: Blessed and full of grace, a source of good fortune for others

women.approach: “So-” echoes si (thought, intelligence); hua suggests literary brilliance and cultural refinement
women.sense: Quick-minded and brilliant, radiating cultivated beauty

women.approach: The compound surname Ouyang echoes “Ol-”; ling brings a lively, spirited quality
women.sense: Cultured elegance, with a mind that dances and leaps

women.approach: Classic transliteration retained; an alternative meaning-based pairing is 安雅 (An Ya)
women.sense: Poised and refined, quietly beautiful

women.approach: “La-” echoes lan (mountain mist); ruo (like, as) suggests the softness of flowing water
women.sense: Mountain mist at dawn, naturally lovely

women.approach: “Ju-” echoes jun (fine jade, gem-like); ya means cultured and pristine
women.sense: Warm as jade, cultured and otherworldly

women.approach: “Em-” echoes en (kindness, grace); yi conveys a contented, joyful ease
women.sense: Deep in kindness, at ease in the joy of living

women.approach: “Mi-” echoes mei (beautiful, fine); ya adds elegance beyond the ordinary
women.sense: Beautiful and refined, gentle and captivating

women.approach: “Cha-” echoes chan, as in chan juan (the moon, graceful beauty); yue means delight and joy
women.sense: Moonlike purity and grace, joyful and radiant

women.approach: “Vi-” echoes wei (resilient, pliant); qing suggests moral clarity and bright transparency
women.sense: Resilient and yielding, clear and transparent

women.approach: “El-” echoes li (beautiful, elegant); ze brings the image of nurturing moisture and generous grace
women.sense: Graceful and lovely, her kindness touching all around her

women.approach: “Je-” echoes jia (fine, commendable); si brings deep thought and intellectual quickness
women.sense: A fine reputation, the mind nimble and bright

women.approach: “Jen-” echoes jia (excellent, beautiful); ning conveys tranquility and settled calm
women.sense: Fine and serene, warm and unhurried

women.approach: “Am-” echoes an (at ease, peaceful); man suggests a leisurely pace without urgency
women.sense: At ease in herself, moving through life with unhurried grace

women.approach: “Me-” echoes mei (beautiful); xia evokes rosy dawn clouds
women.sense: Beautiful as morning clouds, brilliant and eye-catching

women.approach: “Ni-” directly gives the surname-character Ni; jie means pure in character and unstained
women.sense: Quietly elegant, with a character that stays clean

women.approach: “Ste-” echoes si (thought); ning brings stillness and freedom from distraction
women.sense: Thoughtful and reflective, at peace in the depths

women.approach: “Ra-” echoes ru (like, as); cheng suggests crystalline clarity
women.sense: Clear as still water, pure and transparent

women.approach: “Han-” echoes han (to hold, to contain); ya brings classical elegance
women.sense: Deep in cultivation, gracious and dignified

women.approach: “Ka-” echoes jia (fine); jin is a rare and precious jade
women.sense: Fine and precious, warm as jade

women.approach: “Lau-” echoes lan (mountain mist); yun brings poetic rhythm and resonance
women.sense: Mist drifting freely, with the lingering cadence of verse

women.approach: “He-” echoes he (harmony, accord); ya suggests refined elegance
women.sense: Harmonious and at peace, elegant in bearing

women.approach: “Ca-” echoes chan (graceful, moon-like); ting evokes a tranquil courtyard
women.sense: Moon-like in purity, a quiet courtyard of refined charm

women.approach: “Re-” echoes ru (like, as); bi is jade-green freshness
women.sense: Clear as green jade, naturally and purely beautiful

women.approach: “Di-” echoes dan (vermilion, loyalty); ya brings cultured elegance
women.sense: Loyal at heart, refined and above the ordinary

women.approach: “Chr-” echoes qing (clear, fresh); chen is morning, first light, and beginner’s heart
women.sense: Fresh and clear, full of morning energy and original spirit

women.approach: “San-” echoes shan as in shan hu (coral, vivid beauty); ruo adds softness and natural ease
women.sense: Vivid as coral, softly alive and graceful

women.approach: “Ca-” echoes jia (excellent, beautiful); ruo evokes the Daoist quality of going with the natural flow
women.sense: Fine in every way, at ease in the flow of things

women.approach: “Li-” echoes li (beautiful, elegant); si brings a quick and resourceful intelligence
women.sense: Beautiful and poised, with an agile and searching mind

scorecard.question: Does it fit who you are and what you reach for?
Does it ride the momentum of your name and situation?
Are length, scope, and bearing well-proportioned — not cramped, not overfull?
Does the Chinese name echo your original, and read smoothly?
Are the characters upright, easy to write and read, not obscure?
Does the meaning truly fit your aims and character?
Does it leave an image in the mind — a scene, an atmosphere?
Does its surname give you roots in the world of the characters?
Have homophones, dialect, and gender mismatches been screened?
Does it carry a wish you would gladly receive?
Is there a literary source — graceful, not obscure?
Taken as a whole, is it truly you?

candidate_types.character: Easy, memorable, hard to get wrong
A touch more allusion and atmosphere
candidate_types.best_for: Those happy to tell their name’s story
Those who want a name heard once, never forgotten

profession_fit.favored: 信, 诚, 正, 达, 衡
睿, 捷, 新, 致, 远
文, 思, 知, 谦, 明
仁, 康, 济, 和, 宁
澈, 霁, 川, 墨, 逸

sound_bridge.note: Final syllables often silent; take the stressed one (Henri → the “ri” sound)
Long compounds; take one of two syllables, or the first and last sounds
Rolled r and ll have no Chinese match; approximate, don’t force
Many syllables; subtract boldly, keep two or three
Where the name already uses Chinese characters, carry the meaning across rather than transliterate

middle_name.do: Set it aside — leave it out (the default, and the most natural)

middle_name.if: A name you actually go by
middle_name.do: Anchor the sound on it, not on your first name
Carry the meaning, not the sound — fold it into one character

middle_name.if: Something you want shown with the first
middle_name.do: Use a three-character name, one character for each
Treat as a thread of lineage that informs the surname

character_tiers.use: Suits almost anyone, hardest to get wrong
character_tiers.samples: 安 an, 和 he, 明 ming, 诚 cheng, 清 qing, 文 wen, 嘉 jia

character_tiers.use: More allusion; best for those who know the sense
character_tiers.samples: 远 yuan, 澈 che, 霁 ji, 谦 qian, 毅 yi, 知 zhi

character_tiers.use: Vivid, hard to carry; rarely for first-timers
character_tiers.samples: 龙 long, 雄 xiong, 巍 wei

digital_modules.output: Maps your name’s sounds into Chinese
A shareable explanation and usage guide
